title: Organic Farming Awareness
intro: The objective of this guide is to provide Rajasthan farmers with simple, practical, and step-by-step information for doing chemical-free (organic) farming in a safe and profitable way.
sections: Organic farming is a farming system in which chemical fertilizers, pesticides, and herbicides are not used.
Only natural inputs and biological methods are used to maintain soil fertility and protect crops in organic farming.
Farmyard manure, compost, vermicompost, green manure, and traditional organic solutions are applied regularly.
Organic farming keeps soil, crops, water, animals, and human health safe and improves long-term productivity.
Organic farming is very useful in Rajasthan because rainfall is low and water is scarce in many areas.
Continuous and heavy use of chemical fertilizers and pesticides has reduced soil fertility and soil organic matter.
The cost of cultivation becomes high in chemical farming due to repeated purchase of fertilizers and pesticides.
Demand and market price of organic products are increasing in cities and export markets, giving better income to farmers.
Apply well-decomposed farmyard manure (FYM) and compost before sowing to prepare healthy, living soil.
Use vermicompost to improve soil structure, water-holding capacity, and nutrient availability in sandy soils.
Select local or indigenous varieties and seeds that are more tolerant to drought and local pests and diseases.
Treat seeds with Trichoderma or other bio-agents to protect them from seed- and soil-borne diseases.
Use Jeevamrut, neem cake, vermicompost, and other organic inputs regularly for nutrient management.
Irrigate crops according to crop stage and soil moisture, and avoid excess water to save water and prevent disease.
Control weeds through timely manual weeding, mulching with crop residues, and planned crop rotation.
Prepare neem oil spray by mixing neem oil, water, and a small amount of liquid soap or emulsifier for effective pest control.
Use Trichoderma, good drainage, and proper spacing to manage crop diseases and reduce fungal infection.
Follow crop rotation such as Wheat → Moong and Bajra → Chana to maintain soil fertility and break pest-disease cycles.
Suitable organic crops for Rajasthan include Bajra, Moong, Chana, Cumin, Coriander, Isabgol, and other arid-region crops.
Important organic certification options for farmers include Jaivik Bharat (PGS/NPOP-based logo for organic food) and NPOP certification for export-oriented production.
